purpose: Phase 1 of !p_track — Planning: deep analysis and decomposition. Documents Steps 1-11 of the master's pre-dispatch flow: Step 1 (resolve {project_root} + read CLAUDE.md + toc.md + additional context dirs), Step 2 (read tracker_master_instructions.md before any write), Step 3 (parse the prompt — extract task name as kebab-case slug + affected directories), Step 4 (deep analysis incl. 4A consult dep_graph.json), Step 5 (read all relevant context files in parallel + 5A build the convention_map), Step 6 (decompose into atomic tasks grouped into logical waves; 6 sub-steps including dep-graph coupling check, decomposition cost-benefit heuristic, context budget check, token budget estimate, and convention relevance checklist), Step 7 (Waves vs Chains decision), Step 8 (write plan.json — the canonical planning artifact with required schema, validate-plan-required.sh hook enforcement, and deep-thinking `approach` requirement), Step 10 (verify deps + topological sort + critical path + bottleneck detection + dep-graph validation), Step 10B (verify full-tracking thresholds), Step 11 (select dashboard via 11-PRE pre-assigned-dashboard rule + 11A archive-and-clear HARD GATE + 11B write initialization.json + 11B-validate dependency graph + 11C write logs.json initialization entry + dashboard-id-survival-rule for compaction + 11D present plan + 11E NON-NEGOTIABLE approval gate). Step 9 was removed (legacy parallel_*.json deprecated).
gotchas: plan.json is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for shared swarm context + per-task instructions. validate-plan-required.sh hook BLOCKS initialization.json writes when plan.json is missing, malformed, or incomplete.
Required plan.json fields enforced by hook: top-level name+created, context.prompt non-empty, tasks[] length≥1, per-task id+title+description+approach+files (≥1 entry).
agents.length MUST equal tasks.length — hook cross-checks when both files are written.

`approach` is NOT a paraphrase of `description` — it is the master's deep-thought how-to (algorithm choices, data flow, file ordering, hazards). Stub approaches corrupt every downstream worker prompt; the hook accepts thin approaches but quality matters.
Step 9 was REMOVED — legacy tasks/{MM_DD_YY}/parallel_*.json/parallel_plan_*.md are deprecated. plan.json carries all planning data; lifecycle data lives ONLY in worker progress files.
Step 11A is a HARD GATE — archive-then-clear-then-VERIFY (`ls progress/` MUST be empty) before proceeding to 11B. Skipping verify can leave ghost progress files.
Pre-assigned dashboards are unconditional — no scanning, no auto-selection. Empty → set up directly; non-empty → auto-archive (no user confirmation) and proceed; never use a different dashboard.

11E approval gate is NON-NEGOTIABLE — write `permission`-level log entry to trigger dashboard popup, present the explicit prompt 'Ready to execute. Approve to begin dispatching {N} agents?', then HALT. Do not dispatch, do not write master_state.json, do not begin Phase 2.

Acceptable approval triggers: explicit 'yes/go/approved/proceed/do it/lgtm/looks good'. Modifications: apply to plan.json first then propagate to initialization.json, re-present, request approval again.

DASHBOARD ID survival: system prompt wraps DASHBOARD ID in `===DASHBOARD_BINDING_START===`/`===DASHBOARD_BINDING_END===` sentinel markers. After compaction, MUST verify the line is still present; if missing, STOP, attempt recovery from initialization.json/parent dir name, ask user as last resort. NEVER auto-select a different dashboard.

Token budget hard limit per worker prompt: 8000 tokens (~32KB text). Section budgets: CONVENTIONS ≤200 lines, REFERENCE CODE ≤100 lines, UPSTREAM RESULTS ≤50 lines/dep, CONTEXT ≤150 lines, total ≤800 lines.

Decomposition cost-benefit: if splitting a task doesn't reduce critical path by ≥20%, MERGE it back. 20 tiny tasks cost more in orchestration overhead than 4 medium ones. Sweet spot: 4-8 tasks per swarm, 10-15 for cross-repo.
If 500+ line CLAUDE.md, ALWAYS summarize (not quote) — extract 5-10 most relevant rules as bullets.
Convention Relevance Checklist (Step 6D): apply per-worker — different tasks need different convention subsets. Filter by task action types (creates files? touches imports? modifies tests? etc.).
Dep graph coupling check (Step 6 + Step 10 dep graph validation) catches HIDDEN coupling that task-level analysis misses — concurrent tasks modifying tightly-coupled files cause merge conflicts.

Dependency validation algorithm: topological sort (catches cycles) + critical-path depth computation + bottleneck detection (any task in 3+ depends_on lists) + orphan check (every depends_on ID exists, every task reachable from a root).
patterns: 11-step planning flow: resolve roots → read instructions → parse → deep analysis (with dep_graph) → read context (with convention_map) → decompose (with budget+relevance checks) → choose Waves/Chains → write plan.json (hook-validated) → verify deps → check tracking thresholds → select+populate dashboard → present plan → approval gate.

plan.json schema: {name, created, context: {prompt, project_root, tracker_root, dashboard_id, type, directories, conventions: convention_map, reference_code[], architectural_decisions, edge_cases, shared_constraints}, tasks[]: {id, title, wave, depends_on, directory, description, approach, files[], context, critical, success_criteria[], instruction_mode, tags[]}}.
convention_map categories: naming, file_structure, imports, frontend_styling, backend_api, error_handling, testing, types — held in working memory, used to filter per-worker.
Convention Relevance Checklist (Step 6D) — Include When/Skip When per-category cross-referenced against each task's files+description.
Dependency validation algorithm: topological sort + depth computation + bottleneck detection + orphan check.
Approval Gate 4-step protocol: write `permission` log entry → present explicit prompt → HALT → on approval log info entry and activate eager dispatch.
Dashboard claiming follows pre-assigned-binding-is-absolute rule (no scanning, no auto-selection).
Archive-then-clear-then-verify HARD GATE before initialization.json write.
conventions: Tables for convention_map categories, decomposition heuristics, context budget, token budget per section, convention relevance checklist, full-tracking thresholds.
Inline JSON examples with `// matches initialization.json` cross-reference comments in jsonc.
PATH SAFETY callout blockquotes before every write step.
Step 9 retained as a placeholder noting its removal/migration.
